description_long: This is a standard hand screwdriver featuring a hexagonal, contoured handle made of blue and black plastic, designed for a comfortable and secure grip. The handle is relatively thick, with flat facets for anti-roll stability and ergonomic handling. The shaft is made of dark metal, extending straight from the handle and terminating in a pointed tip, which appears to be a Phillips type. The blue plastic of the handle is accented with black patches, and there is visible white text or branding on one of the handle's facets. The overall construction suggests durability and suitability for manual fastening tasks in urban environments such as garages, workshops, or maintenance settings. The object appears clean and well-maintained, with no visible signs of wear or damage.
description: A blue and black contoured-handle screwdriver with a dark metal Phillips tip and visible branding. The handle is hexagonal and ergonomic, designed for a secure grip.
description_view_0: The screwdriver is seen from a slight angle, showing the blue and black contoured handle and the dark metal shaft.
description_view_1: A direct view of the handle's facet with visible white branding and the full length of the shaft and tip.
description_view_2: The handle's side is visible, showing the blue and black color pattern and a partial view of the branding.
description_view_3: A straight-on view of the handle's opposite facet, with the shaft and tip centered below.
attribute_handle_type: Hexagonal, contoured, blue and black plastic handle with flat facets for grip and anti-roll stability.
